item.title: Understanding the LLM Mobile Landscape in Enterprise Technology
item.description: Learn how threat actors can manipulate LLM technology to create convincing social engineering schemes, and how you can protect your organization.

item.title: What Is a Large Language Model (LLM)?
item.description: Learn about large language models (LLMs), key concepts such as transformers and training data, and the opportunities and risks they present to organizations.

item.title: How to Stay Current with Changing Security Landscape: Feat. Head of Security from BNP Paribas
item.description: I spoke with Ramy Houssaini, Chief Cyber and Technology Risk Officer at BNP Paribas, about the challenges CISOs face in an increasingly complex digital landscape.

item.title: LLM Security Checklist: Essential Steps for Identifying and Blocking Jailbreak Attempts
item.description: Learn how to defend your organization’s sensitive large language model (LLM) from jailbreaking attempts by following a simple five-step checklist.

item.title: A Security Work Stream Is Critical to IT Modernization
item.description: IT cloud modernization is a great way to drive sales growth, have the edge over your competitors, and boost your employees’ productivity while enabling greater collaboration.

item.title: Is Your Mobile Security Stuck in the Past?
item.description: Modern mobile threats have moved beyond the device. If your strategy hasn’t evolved, your risk exposure has.

item.title: Holiday Season Cyber Attack Patterns: How They Impact Enterprise Operations
item.description: Learn how cyber attackers target enterprise-level organizations during the holidays — and what proactive steps you can take to mitigate the threat.

item.title: Human Risk Multiplier: How Mobile Devices Expand Enterprise Attack Surfaces
item.description: Threat actors use mobile devices to exploit human psychology. Enterprise organizations can devise effective countermeasures.

item.title: Seasonal Phishing Scams: Protect Your Enterprise During the Holiday Spike
item.description: The holidays are a busy time for cyber attackers. Learn about common phishing scams and how to prevent them during this hectic season.

item.title: Mobile App SDKs: The Nesting Dolls of Hidden Risk
item.description: With the number of devices being used for work everyday — especially as more employees bring their own devices — it’s impossible for security and IT...

item.title: FraudGPT and the Future of Cyber crime: Proactive Strategies for Protection
item.description: Protect your organization from FraudGPT and other adversarial LLMs. Use AI to enhance your threat detection and train your employees to spot likely threats.

item.title: Ethical and Regulatory Implications of Agentic AI: Balancing Innovation and Safety
item.description: Learn about the ethical implications of agentic AI, and how policymakers, technologists, and ethicists can collaborate on this promising new tool.

item.title: The Ultimate Guide to Enterprise Data Protection Solutions
item.description: Staying ahead of bad actors requires taking a comprehensive view of data security, integrity, and availability.

item.title: Energy Industry Faced with 161% Surge in Mobile Phishing
item.description: To help the energy industry react to evolving cyber threats, Lookout today published the 2021 Lookout Energy Industry Threat Report.

item.title: Using Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) in Mobile Defense
item.description: Traditional endpoint detection and response isn’t complete without a mobile component. Read to learn how mobile EDR software helps protect your organization.

item.title: The Role of Digital Forensics and Incident Response (DFIR) in Cybersecurity
item.description: Learn how to develop a comprehensive digital forensics and incident response (DFIR) strategy, which can help your organization recover after a data breach.

item.title: Credential Theft Protection: Defending Your Organization's Data
item.description: Credential theft is one of the most common ways for threat actors to get into your systems. Learn what it is and how to protect against it.

item.title: The Automated Con: Mitigation Tactics for Identifying Deepfake and LLM-Assisted Impersonation
item.description: Learn how employees at your organization can identify deepfake video, audio, images, and text in order to thwart convincing phishing schemes.

item.title: How a Financial Services Firm Prevents Email Data Leakage with Lookout
item.description: This large financial services firm needed to ensure they were protecting data in email without increasing complexity or limiting productivity.

item.title: Attackers Wielding DarkSword Threaten iOS Users | Threat Intel
Cybersecurity Is Not Complete Without EDR for Mobile
item.description: Businesses are increasingly turning to mobility solutions to increase productivity. You need mobile EDR to provide visibility across all of your mobile endpoints.

item.title: The Rise of Holiday Cyber Threats: What Organizations Need to Know
item.description: Learn how to protect your organization from cyber threats over the holidays.

item.title: Three Lessons from the Confluence Server Vulnerability (CVE-2022-26134)
item.description: The vulnerability would allow an unauthenticated malicious actor to execute arbitrary code on a Confluence Server or Data Center instance that could grant full command.

item.title: CMMC Mobile Security: A Guide to Compliance for Enterprise Organizations
item.description: Ensure that your organization complies with CMMC mobile security standards. Enforce smart mobile device policies and maintain a strong cybersecurity framework.

item.title: Lookout Helps a Large Oil Company Migrate to Cloud-Based Software
item.description: Lookout helped to implement granular access control for sensitive HR data, achieve compliance, and prevent malware from being uploaded to their infrastructure.

item.title: BYOD Increases Mobile Phishing Rates, and the Risks Have Never Been Higher
item.description: We've just published our Global State of Mobile Phishing report, which explores phishing trends and the risk they present to both your data and your financial bottom line.

item.title: Attention CISOs: the Board Doesn’t Care About Buzzwords
item.description: We live in an IT world surrounded by buzzwords that are largely marketing gimmicks. Zero Trust, for example, is a concept no one understands and is slapped onto everything.

item.title: The Double-Edged Sword: Benefits and Risks of AI Transformations
item.description: While AI tools can foster innovation and improve efficiency, they can also help craft phishing schemes and spread malware.

item.title: Beyond BEC: How Modern Phishing Has Evolved Past Email
item.description: Business email compromise (BEC) is big business for malicious actors. According to the 2021 FBI Internet Crime Report, BEC was responsible for $2.4 billion in cyber crimes.

item.title: What Is Lockdown Mode for iOS and iPadOS and Why Should I Care?
item.description: We welcome the introduction of Lockdown Mode by Apple as an additional feature. However, we need to keep in mind that the mobile attack surface is quite complex.

item.title: Android and The Sideloading Security Gap
item.description: For years, debates about Android security have essentially focused on the wrong questions. Is the operating system secure enough? Are mobile exploits becoming more sophisticated? Are app stores doing enough to screen malicious software?

item.title: Protecting the Human Layer: Lookout Smishing AI Takes on Next-Gen Social Engineering Threats
item.description: In today's cybersecurity landscape, the most sophisticated attacks often don't target firewalls or servers—they target people. As threat actors leverage AI to craft increasingly convincing social engineering campaigns, employees have become the front line of defense. The mobile device, ever-present and often a repository of personal and professional information, is the primary battleground.

item.title: Agentic AI has Arrived on Employee Mobile Devices
item.description: Agentic Artificial Intelligence is evolving at a blistering pace, and so are the risks that come with it. The latest generation of agentic AI mobile apps can connect to corporate systems, collect data, and make autonomous decisions with minimal human intervention. They aren’t science fiction—they’re already on personal and business devices today, ranging from consumer convenience tools to enterprise-grade assistants.

item.title: Adversarial AI and Polymorphic Malware: A New Era of Cyber Threats
item.description: AI-powered malware looks to change the cybersecurity landscape as we know it. Here’s how you can stay ahead of the curve.

item.title: Account Takeover Protection: What It Is and How It Combats ATO Fraud
item.description: Protect your organization from account takeovers by recognizing common attacks, educating your staff, and implementing the right software solutions.

item.title: A New Chapter in Mobile Security: Tackling Human Risk with AI-Powered Social Engineering Protection
item.description: This week marks a milestone in the evolution of mobile endpoint security. At a time when attackers are moving faster and targeting smarter, Lookout is proud to unveil a breakthrough initiative: AI-powered social engineering protection—the first solution of its kind built to detect and disrupt human-targeted attacks at the mobile edge.

item.title: Cybersecurity Forecast: Key Predictions for 2025
item.description: As cyber attackers adopt more sophisticated tactics, techniques, and procedures, organizations will have to be forward-thinking to fortify their defenses.

item.title: Financial Sector Phishing Attacks Increase 125%
item.description: To better understand how the financial industry reacted to this digital shift, I took a deep dive into the millions of device and threat data in the Lookout Security Graph.

item.title: Critical Mobile Security Capabilities Everyone Needs
item.description: While away from the office, your workers are using their mobile devices to stay productive. So how do you secure your data in this new reality?

item.title: Lookout Helps Leading Fintech Company Improve Data Security and Compliance
item.description: See how Lookout helped a leading FinTech provider protect customer data and reach security compliance using Secure Cloud Access with DLP.

item.title: Top Mobile App Security Risks: From Risky Behavior to Hidden Malicious Code
item.description: TikTok to Pinduoduo and Temu are just some of the most recent examples of mobile app risks. Read this blog to learn about how you can protect your organization from them.

item.title: Enhancing Mobile Device Security: Applying the NIS2 Directive
item.description: In response to a changing threat landscape, the EU's NIS2 directive recommends mobile threat defense (MTD) solutions.

item.title: Privacy vs. Mobile Security: Why You Don’t Have to Choose
item.description: Many people are resistant to putting an employer-mandated security app on their phone, but organizations don't have to sacrifice security for privacy.

item.title: Navigating BYOD Security: Proven Strategies and Best Practices for Success
item.description: Employees rely on their personal devices to get work done. A sensible BYOD security policy can help safeguard your organization’s data.

item.title: 5 Essential Tips to Prevent Social Engineering
item.description: Social engineering, unlike traditional hacking that targets technical vulnerabilities, manipulates human psychology

item.title: Security Made Easy: Mobile Security That Won’t Stretch Your IT Team
item.description: Discover how Lookout simplifies mobile security with easy deployment and management, enhancing IT efficiency without added strain.

item.title: Human-Directed Threats: The New Frontline in Cybersecurity
item.description: A constantly shifting threat landscape has given rise to a new cyberattack vector.

item.title: Navigating FedRAMP Compliance: Why It’s Crucial for Mobile Security
item.description: Learn how ensuring FedRAMP compliance can protect mobile devices and foster strong relationships between private organizations and the U.S. federal government.

item.title: Protecting Government Agencies From Mobile Threats: A Guide
item.description: Comply with federal government standards — particularly FedRAMP and the CDM Program — to protect your agency’s sensitive data from threat actors.

item.title: How the CDM Program Strengthens Federal Cyber Resilience
item.description: Since its creation in 2012, the CDM Program has helped mitigate cyber threats to government networks — here’s how.
